commit.message:
[loop invariants] fix loop invariant with function calls in && chain (#3741)

## Description

Fixes #3735

Loop invariants of the form `__ESBMC_loop_invariant(A && f(x) && B)`
(function call in the middle of `&&`) were mis-handled: the generic
`remove_sideeffects` path turned the expression into short-circuit GOTO
and dropped guard `A` from the stored invariant, leading to wrong
verification results.

**Fix:**

1. **goto_sideeffects.cpp** — Special-case `__ESBMC_loop_invariant`:
flatten the top-level `&&`, run `remove_sideeffects` per conjunct,
rebuild the `and` chain. The full expression is preserved in the
LOOP_INVARIANT instruction.
2. **goto_loop_invariant.cpp** — Extract DECL/FUNCTION_CALL instructions
for invariant temporaries into a side-effects block and re-insert it
before each ASSERT/ASSUME so calls are re-evaluated after HAVOC.
3. Loops with no modified variables are no longer skipped; the base-case
ASSERT is still emitted to catch invalid invariants.

**Tests:** `github_3735` (all_cases.c: fun first/middle/last in `&&`),
`github_3735_fail` (expect FAILED), `github_3735_multi_fc` (two FCs in
`&&`).

---------

Co-authored-by: LukeW1999 <182648641+LukeW1999@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: remove crossOrigin and prevent GC of in-flight Image objects

Two root causes identified for PNG assets never rendering:

1. crossOrigin='anonymous' on new Image() fails in VS Code webviews
   because the vscode-resource server may not send CORS headers.
   The retry mechanism (load without crossOrigin after CORS failure)
   could also fail due to browser response caching. Fix: remove
   crossOrigin from ALL image loads — tilesets only need drawImage()
   (works on tainted canvases), and character sheet removeBackground()
   already catches SecurityError and falls back to raw image.

2. Image objects created as local variables could be garbage collected
   before onload fires, especially under memory pressure from 7+
   simultaneous multi-MB loads. Fix: add module-level _pendingImages

commit.message:
feat: add update-env workflow to update .env without full setup (#31)

## Problem
Updating the `.env` file on the server required running the full
`setup-server` workflow, which also installs packages, configures
Docker, firewall, etc.

## Solution
A lightweight manual workflow that only writes the `.env` file.

## Changes

### `.github/workflows/update-env.yml` (new)
- `workflow_dispatch` only — no automatic triggers
- Same `testing` / `production` environment input as `setup-server`
- Runs the Ansible playbook with `--tags env` → only executes the "Write
.env file" task
- No bootstrap/deploy step

### `ansible/playbooks/setup-server.yml`
- Added `tags: [env]` to the "Write .env file from secrets" task so it
can be targeted independently

## Usage
GitHub → Actions → **Update Server .env** → Run workflow → choose
environment.

commit.message:
PercentageView: fix xmark buttons — scope GlassEffectContainer per card

Previous attempt (ZStack inside a shared GlassEffectContainer) did not
work because GlassEffectContainer composites ALL its descendants into one
rendering pass, collapsing ZStack z-ordering within that scope.

Root cause identified: the outer GlassEffectContainer wrapped the entire
ScrollView, so even a ZStack above the glass card was still inside the
glass compositing context, rendering the button underneath the material.

Fix: remove the single full-screen GlassEffectContainer; each element
now uses its own GlassEffectContainer scoped to just that card. The
clear button lives in a ZStack OUTSIDE the card's GlassEffectContainer,
so it is genuinely above the glass layer in the render tree.

Also added previewRate/previewValue init params (default empty) to allow
rendering the filled state in previews for visual verification.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: remove useRuntimeConfig from module-level axios setup (#3)

## Problem

`api/index.ts` called `useRuntimeConfig()` at module initialisation time
via a `getApiBase()` helper. This runs before the Nuxt plugin context is
ready on the server, causing:

```
[Vue Router warn]: uncaught error during route navigation:
Error: [nuxt] instance unavailable
    at useNuxtApp
    at useRuntimeConfig
    at index-DV9GKTDj.mjs:39
```

## Fix

Remove `getApiBase()` entirely and reference
`process.env.NUXT_PUBLIC_API_BASE` directly in the `axios.create()`
call.

- On the **server** (SSR): the env var is available as a real process
environment variable.
- On the **client**: Nuxt/Vite inlines the value at build time (same
source as `runtimeConfig.public.apiBase` in `nuxt.config.ts`).

No behaviour change — the fallback `http://localhost:8080` is preserved.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
PercentageView: fix clear button hidden behind glass — use ZStack above GlassEffectContainer

Root cause:
GlassEffectContainer composites all children into a single glass
rendering pass. .overlay() on a card child was drawn within that
compositing context, so subsequent cards' glass surfaces rendered
on top of it — making the button visually and hit-test invisible.

Fix:
Introduced cardRow(field:text:) which wraps each inputCard in a
plain ZStack(alignment: .trailing). The clear button is placed as
the ZStack's top layer, completely outside the glass compositing
context, guaranteed to render above all glass surfaces.

inputCard: removed .overlay and Color.clear spacer; replaced with
a slim Color.clear(width:36) spacer in the HStack so text doesn't
run under the xmark position.

Button styling: white foreground + subtle black shadow so the icon
is clearly readable over any glass tint colour.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
